Town leadership has known how dangerous Harvard Street is for cyclists for decades and presumably knew it was due for paving. Yet, it appears the town failed to contemplate even basic incremental protective safety improv

We also fully support the town's adoption of vision zero, which is an approach to road safety that says that the status quo is not okay. It challenges decades of transportation policy that prioritize motor vehicles motor
The vis vision zero plan adopted by the town created a roadmap to achieve its goals of zero fatalities and serious injuries by 2035.

copy.description:
Brookline Select Board Meeting - April 14, 2026

At the Brookline Select Board meeting on April 14, 2026, discussions focused on roadway safety improvements, particularly for Harvard Street, and the importance of incorporating complete streets policies to enhance safety for cyclists and pedestrians [50:24][64:31][115:28]. The board heard public comments supporting a more pedestrian-friendly design and criticized the prioritization of motor vehicle throughput over safety [60:09][67:12][126:48].
